Title: Glandless, a tomato HD-ZIP transcription factor, is important for the gland formation of type VI trichomes

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HD-ZIP I homeobox-leucine zipper transcription factor; homeobox domain and leucine zipper motif
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: specialized metabolites of glandular trichomes implicated in plant defense
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: type VI glandular trichomes for volatile terpenes; leaf surface/trichome-associated acylsugars
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMS mutant mapping; genomic sequencing of mutant allele; VIGS knockdown in Micro-Tom; trichome microscopy/Cryo-SEM; GC-MS metabolite profiling; RNA-seq differential expression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The glandless phenotype was mapped to SlHDZ38 (Solyc09g008810), which carries a T938A intronic SNP creating an aberrant splice acceptor, frameshift, and premature stop codon. 2. VIGS of SlHDZ38 reproduced aberrant type VI trichomes and reduced mono- and sesquiterpene accumulation, validating SlHDZ38 as causal. 3. SlHDZ38 is required for proper glandular head formation of type VI trichomes and also affects densities of type V and VII trichomes. 4. Loss of SlHDZ38 strongly reduces expression of many trichome terpene-associated regulators and terpene synthase genes, while acylsugar-related genes and acylsucrose levels are modestly increased.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: specialized metabolism in glandular trichomes, especially terpene biosynthesis associated with type VI trichome development; indirect effect on acylsugar-associated metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SlTPS9; SlTPS5; SlTPS1; SlTPS10; SlTPS17; SlTPS18; SlTPS39; SlTPS41; SlEOT1; SlEOT2; SlMYC1; SlSCL3; SlJAZ2; SlJAZ4; SlMTR1; SlHD8; SlWO; SlMX1; SlWOX3b; acylsugar biosynthetic genes (unspecified set)
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for engineering glandular trichome morphology and terpene-based defense traits in tomato; deployment should consider pleiotropic effects on trichome densities and possible shifts toward acylsugar metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: reduced type VI terpene production; altered densities of type V and VII trichomes; shift toward higher acylsucrose accumulation in mutant
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: likely functions as an HD-ZIP dimer via leucine zipper; specific complex partners not experimentally validated in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: greenhouse growth; VIGS in Micro-Tom; no specific abiotic/biotic treatment central to metabolite effect
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: hardly expressed in WT leaf tissue including trichomes, but detected in isolated stem trichomes and reduced in glandless stem trichomes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes formation of type VI glandular trichome heads and supports accumulation of volatile mono- and sesquiterpenes; loss increases acylsucrose levels slightly
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional regulation, likely activation and/or maintenance of trichome regulatory and terpene biosynthetic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positively associated with expression of terpene synthases, SlEOT1, SlEOT2, SlMYC1, SlSCL3, SlJAZ2, SlJAZ4, SlMTR1, and SlHD8; negatively associated in stem trichomes with SlWO, SlMX1, SlWOX3b, SlJAZ5, SlJAZ6, and SlJAZ7
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: essential regulator of type VI gland formation and a high-level controller of trichome terpene output
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: TRV-based VIGS using endogenous SlHDZ38 sequence fragment; promoter not specified
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: detected in isolated stem trichomes; low in leaf tissue including trichomes
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The metabolic effects may be partly indirect through altered type VI trichome morphology. The mutant trichome shows some type IV-like characteristics, and acylsugar biosynthesis is increased, but the new trichome type did not stain as acylsugar-accumulating in Rhodamine B assays. Authors place SlHDZ38 high in the trichome regulatory hierarchy.
